name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-31
title: branch_ops.py does not fetch origin/bug-backlog-files before worktree add
description: branch_ops.py calls 'git worktree add .claude/tmp/bug-backlog-files origin/bug-backlog-files' without first running 'git fetch origin bug-backlog-files'. This assumes the remote-tracking ref already exists locally. In any fresh workspace — including chained workspaces where origin is another local repo rather than GitHub — the ref is absent and the worktree add fails with 'fatal: invalid reference'. Fix: add a 'git fetch origin bug-backlog-files' call inside branch_ops before setting up the worktree. This handles both direct-GitHub and chained-workspace topologies, as long as the intermediate has the branch locally. Reproduce: clone or fresh-worktree a repo whose origin points to a local workspace that has bug-backlog-files only as a remote-tracking ref, then invoke any branch_ops read operation.

history.note: Stale on closer reading: branch_ops.py:190-191 already calls 'git fetch origin bug-backlog-files' before 'git worktree add'. The observed failure was actually chained-workspace topology (ws31 -> ws29 -> GitHub, where ws29 only had bug-backlog-files as a remote-tracking ref). That underlying issue is filed as a separate bug.
